narrative: Deterministic discharge posture was ready, but note evidence introduced a discharge-critical contradiction: Narrative evidence documents exertional desaturation and dyspnea, which materially conflicts with resting-only stability assumptions. (citations: cit_001). Pending clinician reassessment, the transition posture should be treated as not_ready.
key_points: Hidden-risk review status: ok; result: hidden_risk_present.
clinical_stability: Exertional oxygen instability not visible in resting structured data (cit_001).
equipment_and_transport: Required home oxygen or discharge equipment unavailable tonight (cit_002, cit_003).
home_support_and_services: No confirmed overnight support for high-friction home environment (cit_004, cit_005).

recommended_actions.action: Hold discharge and repeat exertional respiratory stability assessment before disposition.
Confirm required home equipment and transport logistics are active before discharge.
Confirm overnight support and safe home supervision plan before discharge.

citations.excerpt: O2 sat dropped to 82% on room air after approximately 20 feet and 6 stairs. Patient became visibly dyspneic. Patient stated, "I do not have oxygen at home tonight and my daughter cannot stay." Oxygen vendor cannot deliver concentrator until tomorrow morning. Patient lives alone o
